metadata.official_name: University of Vermont and State Agricultural College

strategic_profile.executive_summary: The University of Vermont (UVM) is a historic public land-grant university and newly minted Carnegie R1 research institution [cite: 24; cite: 98]. Often regarded as a 'Public Ivy,' it is celebrated for its rigorous programs in environmental science, health professions, and business, while maintaining a liberal arts ethos [cite: 4].
strategic_profile.market_position: Public Ivy / Flagship R1 Research University

analyst_takeaways.insight: Acceptance rates have stabilized around 60-65% after a surge in applications, but out-of-state competition remains stiff [cite: 8; cite: 97].
analyst_takeaways.implication: Applicants should leverage Early Action or Early Decision, which offer significantly higher acceptance rates (70-84%) [cite: 97; cite: 161].

analyst_takeaways.insight: UVM has a high out-of-state sticker price ($63k+ COA) but provides generous automatic merit scholarships (up to $25k/year) to competitive applicants [cite: 18; cite: 61].
analyst_takeaways.implication: Top-tier out-of-state students can treat UVM as a financially viable target/likely school if their stats exceed the middle 50%.

analyst_takeaways.insight: UVM's elevation to R1 status in 2025 injects $266M+ into research and development [cite: 1].
analyst_takeaways.implication: Undergraduates will see an explosion in grant-funded lab roles, particularly in STEM and environmental fields.

analyst_takeaways.insight: The student body is heavily out-of-state (80%), highly liberal, and outdoorsy [cite: 1; cite: 155].
analyst_takeaways.implication: Students looking for an eco-conscious, progressive environment with immediate access to skiing and hiking will find a strong fit.

campus_dynamics.social_environment: UVM boasts a vibrant, politically liberal, and LGBTQ-friendly social scene deeply integrated with the city of Burlington and the surrounding natural environment (Lake Champlain, Green Mountains) [cite: 26; cite: 155].
campus_dynamics.transportation_impact: Burlington is highly walkable and bike-friendly, though access to premier ski resorts is greatly facilitated by having a car or utilizing robust student carpools [cite: 5].
campus_dynamics.research_impact: As an R1 institution, UVM offers exceptional undergraduate research integration. Programs like STEM Scholars and the Pre-Medical Enhancement Program provide structured pipelines into high-level research and clinical exposure [cite: 98; cite: 156].

current_status.test_policy_details: UVM is fully test-optional. Standardized scores are not required for admission, and the university has indicated no immediate plans to reinstate a mandate [cite: 59; cite: 100].

longitudinal_trends.notes: Waitlist admits can fluctuate wildly; some sources report 0 waitlist movement for Class of 2029, while CDS data for the 2024 cycle indicates 391 admitted [cite: 6; cite: 8].

gpa.notes: Average high school GPA is reported between 3.75 and 3.85, with recent incoming classes averaging a 4.0 weighted [cite: 1; cite: 3; cite: 136].

colleges.name: College of Arts and Sciences
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Ideal for students interested in humanities, social sciences, or natural sciences who value a traditional liberal arts education within a research university setting [cite: 7].
colleges.student_archetype: Intellectually curious, socially conscious, and collaborative.

majors.name: Psychology
majors.notes: One of the most popular majors at UVM [cite: 27; cite: 31].

majors.name: Biology
majors.notes: Offered in both CAS and CALS [cite: 8].

colleges.name: Grossman School of Business
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: For students aiming for a highly structured business education with strong experiential learning components and networking in Burlington's entrepreneurial ecosystem [cite: 9].
colleges.student_archetype: Driven, professionally focused, and interested in sustainable business practices.

majors.name: Business Administration
majors.special_requirements: Requires 4 years of high school math with high achievement, including at least 1 year beyond Algebra II (trig, precalc, or calc preferred) [cite: 10].
majors.notes: The most popular major at UVM, known for integrating academic rigor with real-world experiential learning [cite: 27; cite: 31].

colleges.name: College of Nursing and Health Sciences
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: For highly competitive students seeking direct-entry clinical nursing or health professions degrees with hands-on hospital experience [cite: 9].
colleges.student_archetype: Compassionate, scientifically minded, and highly disciplined.

majors.name: Nursing
majors.special_requirements: Requires 1 year of high school biology and 1 year of chemistry [cite: 10].
majors.notes: Transferring into Nursing from another UVM college or an external institution is extremely limited due to clinical seat caps [cite: 11].

colleges.name: Rubenstein School of Environment and Natural Resources
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: For students deeply passionate about conservation, ecology, and climate change. It is one of UVM's signature colleges [cite: 12].

colleges.name: College of Engineering and Mathematical Sciences
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Ideal for students seeking robust technical training coupled with UVM's signature focus on complex systems and the environment [cite: 14].

majors.name: Mechanical Engineering
majors.special_requirements: Requires 4 years of high school math including trig or precalc; 1 year chemistry; 1 year physics [cite: 10].
majors.notes: One of the largest graduating engineering majors at UVM [cite: 15].

application_deadlines.notes: Application must be fully complete by Nov 13 [cite: 5; cite: 178].
ED I deadline. Application must be fully complete by Nov 13 [cite: 5; cite: 178].

supplemental_requirements.details: 1 optional short answer of 500 words. Prompts range from 'Why UVM?' to discussing environmental connections, intersectional identities, or a creative prompt combining an ice cream flavor with a social issue [cite: 17].

holistic_factors.specific_differentiators: UVM explicitly looks for students who match its institutional values, particularly regarding sustainability, community impact, and diversity [cite: 97; cite: 100].

major_selection_tactics: Apply Early Action for a mathematical advantage; EA acceptance rates sit around 70% compared to RD's ~55% [cite: 18].

Ensure prerequisite high school coursework strictly aligns with the chosen college (e.g., Physics/Chem for Engineering, Pre-calc for Business) to avoid automatic rejection from those schools [cite: 10].
Weave environmental advocacy or outdoor affinity into the optional supplemental essay, regardless of major, to signal cultural fit [cite: 97; cite: 101].
application_strategy.alternate_major_strategy: UVM allows students to indicate an alternate major on their application. If an applicant does not meet the strict prerequisites for their primary major (e.g., Nursing or Business), they will be considered for their alternate choice [cite: 87; cite: 88].

financials.aid_philosophy: Significant Merit-Based / Need-Blind Status Not Specified

scholarships.name: Green & Gold Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Covers in-state tuition for all four years for Vermont's top high school seniors.

scholarships.name: Presidential Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Awarded to high-achieving first-year out-of-state students.

scholarships.name: Dean's Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Merit-based award for strong out-of-state students.

scholarships.name: Vermont Scholars Award
scholarships.benefits: For admitted Vermont first-year students demonstrating highest academic performance.

scholarships.name: Patrick Family Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: For admitted Vermont first-year students demonstrating outstanding academic performance.

scholarships.name: Justin Morrill Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: For admitted Vermont first-year students demonstrating strong academic performance.

ap_policy.general_rule: A score of 5 earns credit in all areas. Scores of 3 or 4 earn credit in select exams [cite: 67; cite: 68].
ap_policy.usage: Credit is granted for specific UVM courses as determined by department faculty. Students receiving AP credit may not take the equivalent course at UVM [cite: 19].

ib_policy.general_rule: Grades of 5, 6, or 7 on Higher Level (HL) exams earn credit. Standard Level (SL) exams with a score of 5+ are considered for credit starting July 2025 [cite: 70; cite: 71].

transfer_articulation.restrictions: A minimum grade of C is required for transfer credit. Students must earn a minimum of 30 credit hours physically at UVM to earn a degree [cite: 20].

what_it_takes: Strong alignment with UVM's values of sustainability and community
Specific math/science prep for specialized colleges (Business, Nursing, Engineering)
essay_tips: If answering the 'Why UVM?' prompt, connect personal goals to specific UVM offerings; do not just list facts about Burlington [cite: 17].
Use the environment prompt to discuss an authentic connection to nature or sustainability [cite: 17].
Showcase interdisciplinary thinking, matching UVM's liberal arts/research blend [cite: 17].
red_flags: Failing to meet strict high school prerequisites for the specific college applied to (e.g., missing Physics for Engineering) [cite: 10].
Applying to Nursing as a backup or expecting to easily transfer in later (seats are strictly capped) [cite: 11].
insights: UVM's 'Public Ivy' status and Burlington's immense popularity make it highly competitive for out-of-state applicants, despite an accessible overall acceptance rate [cite: 59; cite: 181].
